rows: Primary evaluator — reasoning, response quality, RAI compliance
Secondary evaluator — security compliance, hallucination detection
Tertiary evaluator — cross-validation, safety propagation checks
Consensus synthesis — aggregates judge outputs into final summaries
Low fault detection success rate indicated by only 20% detection (1 out of 5 runs), implying the agent frequently failed to detect container-kill faults leading to a high false negative rate of 80%.
Agent completely failed to detect any faults, as indicated by a fault_detection_success_rate of 0.0 and false_negative_rate of 1.0 across all 5 runs.
Low fault detection success rate of 20%, indicating that the agent failed to detect 80% of disk-fill faults across runs, significantly affecting overall diagnostic performance.

High variability and generally long time to detect and mitigate faults, as shown by large standard deviations (205s detection std dev, 260s mitigation std dev) and wide range of detection times (min 60.77s, max 584.56s), which can delay response and prolong system faults.
The agent’s time to detect faults was highly variable and generally slow, with a mean of 536.6 seconds and a high standard deviation of 404.45 seconds, causing potential delays in fault handling.

Presence of hallucinations detected in 20% of runs (hallucination_detection rate 0.2) with a maximum hallucination score of 0.07, showing occasional generation of inaccurate or fabricated information.
Relatively high and variable time to detect faults, ranging from 1241.5 to 1497.56 seconds (mean ~1364.5s) with considerable standard deviation (106s), leading to slower incident response.

Zero output tokens recorded across all runs, suggesting the agent may not be generating sufficient actionable remediation output or logs for visibility despite evidence of corrective actions and strong reasoning scores.
Limited scope in diagnostic reasoning during run five, with insufficient exploration of alternative root causes beyond DNS-related issues, potentially narrowing diagnostic effectiveness.
Inconsistent token usage with input tokens averaging 370.2 but median 0 and output tokens at zero across runs, suggesting incomplete or missing output generation in some runs.

Enhance fault detection algorithms specifically for container-kill scenarios by improving telemetry signal integration and anomaly thresholds to increase detection success rate and reduce false negatives.
Enhance fault detection algorithms and telemetry integration to improve detection success rate, targeting elimination of false negatives and ensuring faults are identified in a timely manner.

Improve and enhance fault detection algorithms to increase detection success rate substantially by incorporating additional signal sources, anomaly detection heuristics, and cross-metric correlations for disk-fill conditions.

Implement mechanisms to reduce time to detect and mitigate faults, such as optimizing telemetry data collection frequency, accelerating decision workflows, and pre-emptive detection models to handle fault onset more rapidly and consistently.
Optimize detection workflows to reduce detection latency and variability by streamlining data collection and processing pipelines, aiming to lower mean detection times and standard deviation.
Implement stricter hallucination mitigation techniques, such as verification against reliable sources or confidence thresholding, to reduce misinformation occurrences in outputs.
Optimize the fault detection process to reduce detection latency by refining monitoring frequency, event parsing, and alert prioritization to achieve faster fault identification.

Review agent output generation components to ensure detailed, traceable remediation action logs and explanations are produced and captured as output tokens, improving overall transparency and auditability.
Broaden diagnostic hypothesis generation to systematically consider a wider range of potential root causes beyond DNS issues, especially under low-confidence conditions or ambiguous symptoms.
Investigate and fix the cause of zero output tokens despite non-zero input tokens in some runs to ensure consistent and complete response generation and evidence documentation.
